segments.text: A little later Naomi said to Ruth, {
My daughter, don't you think I should find you a husband and a good home?
segments.speaker: little later Naomi

segments.text: Don't ignore the fact that Boaz, whose women you worked with, is closely related to us. Now tonight he will be busy winnowing grain on the threshing floor.
segments.speaker: little later Naomi

segments.text: Have a bath, put on some perfume, wear your best clothes, and go down to the threshing floor—but don't let him recognize you. Once he's finished eating and drinking,
segments.speaker: little later Naomi

segments.text: watch where he goes to lie down. Then go and uncover his feet and lie down. Then he will tell you what to do.
segments.speaker: little later Naomi

segments.text: I'll do everything you told me,
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: said Ruth.
She went down to the threshing floor and did what her mother-in-law had told her to do.
After Boaz had finished eating and drinking, and was feeling contented, he went and lay down beside the grain pile. Ruth quietly approached him, uncovered his feet, and lay down.
Around midnight Boaz suddenly woke up. Bending forward he was surprised to see a woman lying at his feet.
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: he asked.
I'm Ruth, your servant,
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: she replied.
Please spread the corner of your cloak over me, for you are my family redeemer.
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: May the Lord bless you, my daughter,
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: he said.
You are showing even more loyalty and love to the family than before. You haven't gone looking for a younger man, of whatever social status.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So don't worry, my daughter. I will do everything you ask—everyone in town knows you are a woman of good character.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: However, even though I'm one of your family redeemers, there's one who is more closely related than I am.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: Stay here tonight, and in the morning if he wants to redeem you, then fine, let him do it. But if he doesn't, then I promise you in the name of the living Lord, I will redeem you. Lie down here until morning.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So Ruth lay at his feet until morning. Then she got up before it was light enough to recognize anyone because Boaz had told her, {
No one must know that a woman came here to the threshing floor.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: He also told her, {
Bring me the cloak you're wearing and hold it out.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So she held it out and he poured out six measures of barley into it. He helped her put it on her back and she went back to town.
Ruth went to her mother-in-law, who asked her, {
How did it go for you, my daughter?
segments.speaker: Ruth went to her mother-in-law, who

segments.text: So Ruth told her everything that Boaz had done for her.
And he also gave me these six measures of barley,
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: she added.
He told me, ‘You mustn't go home to your mother-in-law empty-handed.’
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: Naomi said to Ruth, {
Wait patiently, my daughter, until you find out how it all works out. Boaz won't rest until he has it settled today.
segments.speaker: Naomi
